workflow_instructions.mode_overview: This mode validates widget packaging readiness, automatically detects new widgets, updates packaging configuration, runs TWX packaging workflows, verifies generated artifacts, and coordinates deployment to BAW servers. It does not serve as the primary mode for widget implementation.

step.details: Determine whether the user wants to validate widgets, package one or more widgets, troubleshoot packaging issues, or deploy a generated toolkit.
Review the available widgets, packaging scripts, toolkit configuration, and any existing output expectations before running commands.

step.action: Detect new widgets and business objects automatically
step.details: When packaging is requested, scan the widgets directory to identify any new widgets that are not yet in the package_baw.py WIDGET_NAMES list. Also scan the business-objects/generated directory for standalone business objects. Automatically add new widgets to ensure all artifacts are included in the package. Business objects are automatically included by the TWXBuilder without configuration changes.
Do not take ownership of widget implementation changes unless the task is explicitly handed to a widget-authoring mode.

phase.name: analysis
phase.description: Analyze packaging readiness and detect new widgets and business objects

steps.step: Scan the widgets directory to identify all available widgets
Compare with the current WIDGET_NAMES list in package_baw.py
Identify any new widgets that need to be added to the packaging configuration
Scan the business-objects/generated directory to identify all business objects
Note: Business objects are automatically included by TWXBuilder - no configuration needed
Validate that all widgets appear structurally ready for packaging
Validate that all business objects have proper JSON structure

phase.name: business_object_registration
phase.description: Register business objects with class IDs before packaging

steps.step: Check if business-objects/generated directory exists and contains business objects
If business objects are found, run the registration process
Execute: python3 register_business_objects.py
Verify that all business objects are registered in toolkit_packager/baw_custom_types.json
Confirm that class IDs are stable and deterministic
Note: Circular dependencies warning is normal and expected for complex business models

phase.important: This step ensures all business objects have unique BAW class IDs before packaging. The registration is idempotent - running multiple times will reuse existing class IDs. Business objects without registered class IDs cannot be properly packaged into TWX files.

phase.name: configuration_update
phase.description: Update packaging configuration with new widgets

steps.step: If new widgets are detected, update package_baw.py WIDGET_NAMES list
Add new widgets in alphabetical order for consistency
Confirm the update was successful
Note: Business objects require no configuration - they are auto-detected

phase.name: packaging
phase.description: Run the packaging workflow with selected template version

steps.step: Execute package_baw.py with the selected template version
Use: python3 package_baw.py
The packager automatically includes all artifacts:
- Custom widgets from widgets/
- Standalone business objects from business-objects/generated/
- Coaches from coaches/
Capture the resulting output path and artifact summary
Verify the package was created successfully
Confirm that all artifact types are included in the TWX
After successful packaging, ask if the user wants to deploy to the BAW server
Provide deployment as a suggested follow-up action
If user confirms deployment, proceed to deployment phase

phase.name: deployment
phase.description: Deploy toolkit to BAW server (if requested)

steps.step: Establish BAW session using login MCP tool
Deploy the TWX artifact using install_container MCP tool
Monitor deployment status using get_queue_status MCP tool
Report deployment results

automatic_artifact_detection.description: When packaging is requested, automatically detect and include new widgets and business objects

widget_detection.step: List all directories in the widgets folder
For each directory, check if it contains a widget/config.json file
Compare the list of valid widgets with WIDGET_NAMES in package_baw.py
Add any missing widgets to WIDGET_NAMES in alphabetical order
Inform the user about newly detected widgets being added

business_object_detection.step: Business objects are automatically detected by TWXBuilder
TWXBuilder scans business-objects/generated/ for all JSON files
Business objects are processed in dependency order
No configuration changes needed - detection is automatic
The packaging log will show all detected business objects

completion_criteria.criterion: All available widgets are detected and included in packaging configuration
All standalone business objects are automatically detected and included
All coaches are automatically detected and included
Output artifacts are clearly reported with file location and size
Deployment option is offered after successful packaging
If deployment is performed, status is tracked and reported
Implementation work is not mixed into the packaging workflow
